reasoning: To resolve YES, there must be over 90 protest events (more than 10 times the baseline of 9.083) in a 30-day window preceding the resolution date. This translates to roughly 3 protests every day, or a massive nationwide wave of protests occurring across dozens of governorates simultaneously. For a country like Egypt, where protests are intensely repressed and heavily regulated by the state, hitting this threshold is extremely unlikely outside of an Arab Spring-style revolution. Over the past year, the country averaged only about 8 protests a month. While rare state-sanctioned protests have occurred recently (such as on March 31, 2025, for Eid-related pro-Palestinian demonstrations and an April 8, 2025 protest at the Rafah border), ACLED codes protests by distinct event location/day, not participant count. A very large protest in a single location still counts as a single event. There has been no evidence in March or April 2025 of the sustained, multi-city daily unrest necessary to reach ~91 events. Since we are already near the first resolution date (April 20), we can confidently predict a low probability of resolving YES. For the later dates, while structural triggers (economic hardship, the Gaza conflict) remain, the probability of a 10x month-over-month spike remains very low. Per instructions, probability uncertainty scales with the forecast horizon.
